channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Intelligence
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news and analysis from Tier 1 sources

item.title: China Sanctions Philippine Defense Chief Over South China Sea Comments
item.description: China announced sanctions against Philippine Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro for "repeatedly making erroneous remarks concerning China" that undermined bilateral ties. The move escalates tensions in the South China Sea dispute.

item.title: Taiwan Vows to Expel Chinese Coast Guard Patrols From Its Waters
item.description: Taiwan condemned Chinese coast guard patrols near its eastern shores as violations of sovereignty and vowed to expel any challengers, raising cross-strait tensions as Beijing asserts jurisdiction claims.

item.title: Northern Ireland Police Fire Plastic Bullets at Race Riots
item.description: Police in Northern Ireland fired plastic bullets to contain race riots, with reinforcements called in from Great Britain as violence escalated amid rising tensions.

item.title: Man Pleads Guilty to Killing Top Minnesota Democrat and Her Husband
item.description: A man pleaded guilty to the political assassination of the top Democrat in the Minnesota House and her husband while posing as a police officer, in a case that shocked state politics.

item.title: Analysis: Is the Iran Ceasefire Over? What Latest US Attacks Tell Us
item.description: A wave of US strikes represents the gravest test yet of the fragile truce with Iran. Analysts examine what happened, what officials are saying, and whether the deal can survive.

item.title: Iran fights to preserve Lebanon as leverage in US settlement talks
item.description: Iran is waging a calculated campaign to keep Lebanon as its last bastion of Mediterranean influence, tying the country to a grand bargain with Washington as it seeks to end Hezbollah's war with Israel on its own terms.

item.title: Oil prices fall as Trump cancels planned Iran strikes, raising peace hopes
item.description: Oil prices settled lower after President Trump canceled plans to strike Iran, raising expectations for a deal to end more than three months of war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Global markets rally on hopes for Iran peace deal
item.description: MSCI global equities index rallied while the dollar and bond yields fell on renewed hopes for Middle East peace after Trump canceled planned strikes against Iran.

item.title: Japanese business sentiment sours as Middle East war hits economy
item.description: Japanese business sentiment turned negative for the first time in four quarters, a government survey showed, reflecting growing economic pain from the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Trump to nominate Jay Clayton as Director of National Intelligence
item.description: President Trump plans to nominate Jay Clayton, the US Attorney for the Southern District of New York, as Director of National Intelligence, following Congress rejection of a FISA extension tied to spy chief appointments.

item.title: IDF Poised for Hezbollah Offensive in Nabatieh
item.description: Israeli military officials say armor and troops have been covertly moved into position for an attack on Hezbollah stronghold in southern Lebanon city of Nabatieh.

item.title: US Sanctions Cuba State Oil Company
item.description: The State Department announced sanctions against Cuba's state-owned oil and gas company Unión Cuba-Petróleo, expanding pressure on the island nation.

item.title: British Arms Dealer Convicted of Selling Fighter Jets to Warlords
item.description: A Londoner and Greek co-defendant were convicted of supplying planes, helicopters, guns, bombs, grenades and missiles to conflict zones around the world.

item.title: UK Defense Secretary John Healey resigns over military spending
item.description: UK Defense Secretary John Healey unexpectedly quit, saying the government is unwilling to spend enough on the military, in a fresh blow to Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

item.title: Trump calls off Iran strikes, says peace deal imminent
item.description: President Trump canceled planned military strikes on Iran at the last minute, saying negotiations with Tehran were heading toward a possible deal that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: World Bank cuts global growth forecast to 2.5% citing Iran war
item.description: The World Bank cut its 2026 global growth forecast to 2.5% due to the Middle East conflict, warning growth could slow to 1.3% if energy supply disruptions prove more severe.

item.title: US House rejects FISA extension, spy powers set to expire
item.description: The Republican-controlled House rejected a short-term extension of foreign surveillance powers sought by President Trump, deepening a political standoff over his appointment of an inexperienced loyalist as top spy chief.

item.title: US-Canada bridge opening delayed after Trump objections
item.description: The opening of a new $4.7 billion bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor has been delayed after President Trump earlier this year threatened to block the crossing.

item.title: China joins Macron-led video call in rare economic talks ahead of G7 summit
item.description: Chinese Vice Premier Zhang Guoqing will attend a video conference hosted by French President Emmanuel Macron on global economic imbalances, days before G7 countries meet in France to discuss how to deal with waves of low-priced Chinese exports entering their markets.

item.title: Iran and Ukraine loom over G7 as France accommodates Trump
item.description: Wars in the Middle East and Ukraine are set to dominate next week's Group of Seven summit, as host France crafts an agenda aimed at projecting unity and avoiding confrontation with U.S. President Donald Trump.

item.title: China Expands Gulf Drone Market Share as Iran War Creates Opening
item.description: The ongoing US-Iran conflict has provided China an opportunity to increase its defense sales to Gulf states, offering cheaper and faster drone deliveries with fewer restrictions than Western suppliers.

item.title: Russia Central Bank Chief Nabiullina Disappears from Public View Amid Resignation Rumors
item.description: Elvira Nabiullina has not been seen in public for over a week, missing the St. Petersburg forum. Reports link her possible resignation to concerns about border closures and martial law.

item.title: Saudi Arabia Lifts Five-Year Ban on Lebanese Imports in Support of Beirut Government
item.description: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman ordered the resumption of Lebanese imports, offering relief to businesses hit by years of Hezbollah-Israel conflict and showing support for Lebanon's embattled government.

item.title: Slovenia lifts entry ban on Netanyahu as new government shifts Israel policy
item.description: Slovenia's new conservative government abolished an entry ban on Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and two of his ministers, rolling back several Gaza-related measures adopted by the previous administration.

item.title: FIDE suspends Russia over chess federation activities in occupied Ukraine
item.description: The International Chess Federation suspended Russia after it failed to relinquish control of chess bodies in five Ukrainian regions captured by Russian forces. Ukraine called it a "historic victory" for the sport.
